channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Fed chair Jerome Powell receives JFK Profile in Courage award
item.description: Former Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell was honored with the "Profile in Courage" award for helping to safeguard the central bank from political pressure from the White House.
item.content:encoded: Former Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell was honored with the "Profile in Courage" award for helping to safeguard the central bank from political pressure from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: A painting created by a Holocaust survivor was nearly lost in Colorado
item.description: Art created by survivors of the Holocaust is becoming more important as time passes and fewer survivors remain. A significant work was recently rescued.
item.content:encoded: Art created by survivors of the Holocaust is becoming more important as time passes and fewer survivors remain. A significant work was recently rescued.
item.dc:creator: Shanna Lewis

item.title: Locals of Indonesia's Raja Ampat weigh the future of nickel mining on their islands
item.description: Indonesia's pristine Raja Ampat islands face a divide. After mine closures sparked tensions, locals are weighing jobs from nickel mining against environmental protection and tourism.
item.content:encoded: Indonesia's pristine Raja Ampat islands face a divide. After mine closures sparked tensions, locals are weighing jobs from nickel mining against environmental protection and tourism.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: New research helps explain how human brains are able decode speech so quickly
item.description: Scientists are learning how the brain extracts discrete words from a continuous stream of sounds.
item.content:encoded: Scientists are learning how the brain extracts discrete words from a continuous stream of sounds.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: The potential consequences of cutting funds for behavioral science
item.description: The Trump administration continues its assault on science. One little-known area that's been neglected in funding this year is federal research into social, behavioral and economic science.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration continues its assault on science. One little-known area that's been neglected in funding this year is federal research into social, behavioral and economic science.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: ABC is acting as an unlikely First Amendment champion
item.description: ABC and its parent company Disney are fighting President Trump's top broadcast regulator, saying his demand to accelerate the review of their eight local stations' license renewal is unconstitutional.
item.content:encoded: ABC and its parent company Disney are fighting President Trump's top broadcast regulator, saying his demand to accelerate the review of their eight local stations' license renewal is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The latest trend in jeans is over 150 years old
item.description: Selvedge denim – a fabric made with heritage techniques – is having a moment. With inflation top of mind for many, discerning consumers are seeking out jeans that are built to last.
item.content:encoded: Selvedge denim – a fabric made with heritage techniques – is having a moment. With inflation top of mind for many, discerning consumers are seeking out jeans that are built to last.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: A nurse became a friendly face for a woman going through chemo
item.description: When his wife was battling cancer and ended up in the emergency room, Mike Lopes was worried she wouldn't recognize anyone there. A nurse made sure there was at least one familiar face caring for her.
item.content:encoded: When his wife was battling cancer and ended up in the emergency room, Mike Lopes was worried she wouldn't recognize anyone there. A nurse made sure there was at least one familiar face caring for her.

item.title: A kid's book newly published in English has a long backstory in the Soviet Union
item.description: The classic Italian children's book The Adventures of Cipollino was translated into English for the first time last year. The book has a surprising backstory in the former Soviet Union.
item.content:encoded: The classic Italian children's book The Adventures of Cipollino was translated into English for the first time last year. The book has a surprising backstory in the former Soviet Union.
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: The long view of the HIV/AIDS crisis in Mozambique
item.description: A medical facility run by a Catholic association from Italy offers historical perspective on the course of the AIDS epidemic in Mozambique, where over 10 percent of the population lives with HIV.
item.content:encoded: A medical facility run by a Catholic association from Italy offers historical perspective on the course of the AIDS epidemic in Mozambique, where over 10 percent of the population lives with HIV.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Jarenwattananon
